title: Dextroamphetamine and Amphetamine: MedlinePlus Drug Information
text:

Other uses for this medicine 
The combination of dextroamphetamine and amphetamine should not be used to treat excessive tiredness that is not caused by narcolepsy.
This medication may be prescribed for other conditions; ask your doctor or pharmacist for more information.
What special precautions should I follow? 
Before taking dextroamphetamine and amphetamine, tell your doctor and pharmacist if you are allergic to amphetamine, 
dextroamphetamine, 
other stimulant medications such as benzphetamine, lisdexamfetamine (Vyvanse Â® ), or methamphetamine (Desoxyn Â® ); any other medications, 
or any of the ingredients in dextroamphetamine and amphetamine preparations. Ask your pharmacist for a list of the ingredients. tell your doctor or pharmacist if you are taking the following medications or have stopped taking them within the past two weeks: monoamine oxidase (MAO) inhibitors including isocarboxazid (Marplan Â® ), linezolid (Zyvox Â® ), methylene blue, phenelzine (Nardil Â® ), selegiline (Eldepryl Â® , Emsam Â® , Zelapar Â® ), or tranylcypromine (Parnate Â® ). some medications should not be taken with dextroamphetamine and amphetamine.
